summary: The ERP system includes an online admission form integrated with a payment gateway (Razorpay) for collecting application fees. The form is available in a test mode for verification before going live. After a student fills out the form and makes the payment, their entry is recorded in the ERP. The system is configured to show the transaction details and generate a receipt. There is a requirement for the system to automatically send the payment receipt and prospectus to the student's email ID upon successful payment.
steps: Access the online admission form (currently in test mode).
Fill in mandatory student details (name, photo, gender, email, phone number).
Make the payment via the integrated payment gateway (e.g., UPI scanner).
Upon successful payment, the student's entry and transaction will be recorded in the ERP.
A receipt will be generated and displayed (and ideally sent to the student's email).
business_rules: The online admission form is integrated with Razorpay for payment.
The form can be set to a test gate for testing payments without actual deductions.
A fixed application fee (e.g., 10,000) is collected through the form.
Payment gateway charges (GST, bank charges) may be added to the application fee.
Admission number is created after the payment is received and processed.
important_notes: The system should send the payment receipt and prospectus to the student's email after successful online payment.
Payment gateway charges need to be clarified and potentially optimized.
Phone number field on the form needs a restriction for up to 12 digits.
questions: How does the online admission form work?
Is the online admission form integrated with a payment gateway?
What happens after a student fills the form and pays online?
Will the student receive a receipt via email after online payment?
Are there any charges for online payments through the admission form?
How can we test the online admission payment process?
